title: if / else if / else
instruction:
Java's `if/else` should feel familiar. One rule: use braces `{}` always — missing braces are a historic source of critical bugs (Apple's 'goto fail' SSL vulnerability was exactly this).

Run this. Try changing the score.
successMessage: 78 → C. Conditions checked top to bottom; the first true one wins. Always use braces — even for single-line bodies. Consistency prevents bugs.

title: Logical operators: &&, ||, !
instruction:
Combine boolean conditions:

- `&&` (AND) — both must be true
- `||` (OR) — at least one must be true  
- `!` (NOT) — flip true/false

Write an access check: grant access if admin, or (verified AND 18 or older).
successMessage: isAdmin was false but (isVerified && age >= 18) was true — access granted. Parentheses make the logic explicit. Always use them when mixing && and ||.

title: Ternary — one-line if/else
instruction:
The ternary operator returns a value based on a condition:

```java
type result = condition ? valueIfTrue : valueIfFalse;
```

Perfect for simple assignments. Don't nest them — it kills readability.
successMessage: Two decisions in two lines. Ternary is perfect for conditional assignments — just keep them simple. A ternary inside a ternary is a warning sign.

title: switch — classic and modern
successMessage: Modern switch expressions: multiple cases with a comma, arrow -> for the result, no break needed. Throwing in a default is good practice — it catches impossible values immediately.

title: switch with yield
hint: case "admin" -> { System.out.println("Audit: admin login"); yield "Full access"; }
successMessage: yield returns a value from a switch block — like return but for switch expressions. Use -> for simple one-liners, yield for multi-statement cases.

title: instanceof and pattern matching
successMessage: Pattern matching instanceof — check and cast in one operation. s is already a String inside the block, no cast needed. This is a Java 16+ feature used heavily with sealed classes.

title: Build a calculator
instruction:
Final challenge — no starter code.

Write a `calculate(double a, String op, double b)` method using a switch expression that:
- `"+"` — adds
- `"-"` — subtracts
- `"*"` — multiplies
- `"/"` — divides (throw `ArithmeticException` if b == 0)
- `"%"` — modulo

Test with 5 different operations.
hint: double result = switch (op) { case "+" -> a + b; case "/" -> { if (b == 0) throw new ArithmeticException(); yield a / b; } ... };

codeChecks.message: Define calculate method with switch on op.
Throw an exception when dividing by zero.
Print results for at least 5 operations.

successMessage: A full calculator with error handling — switch expression, arithmetic, and exception throwing. Chapter 5 done!
